title: Signed compliance-mapping ledger (Phase 5.4)
description: Content-addressable, append-structured ledger of every compliance mapping declared across UC sidecars. Each entry is hashed independently; the ledger root is a merkle-style SHA256 over all sorted entry hashes. A detached signature (produced at release time via GitHub attestations / Sigstore) binds the root to a specific workflow run, commit, and repository.

$schema.description: Optional IDE-tooling hint pointing to this schema.

schemaVersion.description: Major.minor.patch. Bumped on any breaking change to the ledger record structure or hash formulation.

generatedAt.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp of the last deterministic regeneration via scripts/generate_mapping_ledger.py.

catalogueCommit.description: Short or long SHA of the commit whose UC sidecars this ledger was computed from. --check regenerates the ledger at HEAD and compares to this field; a mismatch indicates either a missed regeneration or out-of-band edit.

hashAlgorithm.description: Hash function used for both per-entry canonicalHash and the merkleRoot. Pinned to sha256 for reproducibility; a v2.0.0 schema may introduce sha3-256 or BLAKE3.

canonicalisation.description: Pins how each entry's canonicalHash is computed so third parties can verify independently.

algorithm.description: JSON Canonicalization Scheme (RFC 8785). The ledger generator uses a compatible subset (sorted keys, no whitespace, UTF-8 NFC, IEEE-754 canonical number form). See docs/signed-provenance.md §3.

fieldOrder.description: Ordered list of fields that participate in the canonicalHash. Presence here documents the ledger contract; absence from this list means the field is ledger-metadata and does NOT feed the hash.

entryCount.description: Count of entries; MUST equal entries.length. Primarily a cheap sanity check.

merkleRoot.description: Lowercase hex SHA256 over the concatenation of each entry's canonicalHash, with entries sorted lexicographically by mappingId. Leaf-count does not pad to a power of two; the structure is a sorted-leaf rolling hash (documented in docs/signed-provenance.md §4).

signature.description: Detached attestation over merkleRoot. In normal development the state is `unsigned` and CI treats it as informational; release automation upgrades to `attested` and pins the GitHub attestation URL.

reason.description: Optional free-text note (e.g., 'development iteration' or 'pre-release dry-run').

signer.description: Identity that produced the attestation (e.g., GitHub workflow identity, Sigstore Fulcio subject).

signatureAlgorithm.description: Signature format. Primary flow uses GitHub's attest-build-provenance, which emits a Sigstore bundle.

attestationUrl.description: Public URL to the attestation (GitHub transparency-log anchor). Verifies via `gh attestation verify`.

bundlePath.description: Optional repo-relative path to the Sigstore bundle artifact (e.g., 'data/provenance/mapping-ledger.sig'). Absent when the bundle is only published via GitHub's API.

workflowRef.description: GitHub workflow ref that produced the signature (e.g., '.github/workflows/release.yml@refs/tags/v5.4.0').

runId.description: GitHub Actions run id (string because 64-bit integers are unsafe in JSON).

commit.description: Commit signed. MUST equal the top-level catalogueCommit.

entries.description: All compliance-mapping ledger entries. MUST be sorted lexicographically by mappingId. The audit rejects duplicate mappingIds.

mappingId.description: Stable identifier: '<ucId>::<regulationId>@<regulationVersion>::<clause>::<mode>::<assurance>'. Deterministic from the sidecar fields; never includes commentary, timestamps, or author identity. regulationVersion and clause are free-form strings because real regulation corpora use whitespace, parentheses, §, and other punctuation (e.g., 'Montpellier (2025)', 'Regulation (EU) 2024/1689', '§24', 'Art.5 §2'). The stricter format invariants live on the individual entry fields.

regulationId.description: Regulation slug matching data/regulations.json frameworks[].id. Canonicalised from the UC sidecar's human-readable name via the generator's name→id table (documented in docs/signed-provenance.md §2).

regulationVersion.description: Specific version the clause belongs to (matches regulations.json versions[].version).

clause.description: Clause reference as quoted in the UC sidecar. Trimmed of leading/trailing whitespace; inner whitespace is preserved (the clause grammar treats whitespace as significant in ranges like 'Art. 25 § 2').

mode.description: Mapping mode (same enum as schemas/uc.schema.json compliance[].mode).

assurance.description: Assurance tier (same enum as schemas/uc.schema.json compliance[].assurance).

derivationSource.description: Present when the entry was propagated via the Phase 3.3 derivesFrom graph. Preserves the parent (parentRegulation, parentVersion, parentClause) so auditors can walk the chain. Field names mirror schemas/uc.schema.json compliance[].derivationSource (do not rename).

parentAssurance.description: Assurance tier inherited from the parent before one-step degradation.

inheritanceMode.description: Matches data/regulations.json derivesFrom[].inheritanceMode. 'identity' = UK-GDPR-style byte-for-byte derivative; 'mapped' = CCPA/LGPD/nFADP/APPI style where the clauseMapping table renames numbering.

divergenceNote.description: Optional note copied from data/regulations.json derivesFrom[].divergences[] when the parent clause was flagged as diverging.

firstSeenCommit.description: Short or long SHA of the commit that first introduced this (ucId, regulationId, clause, mode, assurance) tuple. Derived from `git log --diff-filter=A --follow`; falls back to catalogueCommit when git history is unavailable (shallow clones).

lastModifiedCommit.description: Short or long SHA of the most recent commit that modified any ledger-relevant field for this mapping. Tracks mode upgrades (e.g., 'satisfies' → 'detects-violation-of') and assurance lifts.

signoffStatus.description: Snapshot of peer/legal/SME review state for the mapping at generation time. Informational: Phase 5.4 records the state; gating is enforced by the existing Phase 4.5a/4.5b/5.2 audits.

canonicalHash.description: Lowercase hex SHA256 over canonicalisation.fieldOrder fields. Recomputed by the audit; mismatch is a fatal error.

required.description: Whether the mapping's assurance or mode triggers the gate (e.g., legal review is required for any 'full' assurance; SME review is required for any 'detects-violation-of' mode).

status.description: 'grandfathered' = mapping predates the gate's baseline_commit and is exempt; 'pending' = gate is required and open; 'signed' = at least one matching signoff is on file; 'not-required' = gate does not apply.

latestSignoffPr.description: PR reference (or 'direct-commit') of the most recent qualifying signoff.
